expressions.english: to call someone; to make a phone call
expressions.notes: 걸다 = to dial (a phone call); to hang (something)
끊다 = to hang up (a phone); to cut off

expressions.english: It was a prank phone call.
Can I call you at night?
expressions.notes:
밤 = night
-이/여/여도 되다 = to be okay to + V
-이/여/여 주세요 = (polite) Please do + something + for me

expressions.english: Why didn't you call me yesterday?

expressions.notes:
앞으로 = from now on
-지 마세요 = (polite) Don't + V

expressions.english: You should say something when you've called someone.
expressions.notes:
-(으)면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
말 = language; what one says; expression; word; term
말을 하다 = to speak; to talk; to tell; to say

expressions.english: As soon as I picked up the phone, it got cut off.
Whenever I call, the line is busy.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V
-(으)ㄹ 때마다 = whenever + S + V
통화중이다 = the line is busy

expressions.korean: 지금 회의 중이라 전화를 받을 수 없어요.
expressions.english: I am in the middle of a meeting, therefore I can't answer the phone.
expressions.notes:
지금 = now
회의 = meeting
회의 중 = in the middle of a meeting

expressions.korean: 국제 전화를 많이 해서 전화비가 많이 나왔어요.
expressions.english: I made a lot of international phone calls, so I received a big phone bill.
expressions.notes:
많다 = to be a lot
많이 is an adverb form of 많다.
전화비 = phone bill
나오다 = to come out
